rows.supporting_quote: In 2022, Glenwood Springs voters approved a 2.5 percent accommodations tax (ballot measure 2C) to establish a Workforce Housing Fund ... An estimated $1.7 million per year is forecasted to be generated by the tax paid by visitors and lodgers when they stay overnight at hotels, motels, short-term rentals and similar accommodations within Glenwood Springs.

On September 1, 2022, Glenwood Springs City Council voted to refer an accommodations tax question ... The question will appear on the Tuesday, November 8, 2022 ballot ... IMPOSITION AND ASSESSMENT OF A TWO AND ONE HALF PERCENT (2.5%) EXCISE TAX
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext. The original 1,948-1,611 vote margin was dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: The fund can only be used for workforce housing development and can only be used by a person working within city limits, or inside of the 81601 zip code. ... The city council will be in charge of appropriating the funds ... all expenditures will be subject to an annual audit that will be made public.

The WHFAB was commissioned by City Council in 2023 to assist with managing the funds. ... The powers and duties ... To advise City Council on the creation of workforce housing programs using 2C funds.

Glenwood Springs City Council unanimously approved a $3.8 million financial package during its Dec. 4 meeting for the planned Canyon Vista Apartments ... already committed about $2.3 million ... fee backfills totaling $730,640 ... largest single affordable housing investment the city has ever made.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Article date and prior audit context support December 2024; text retained only for fetched dollar/package claims.
